turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Café Roleplay Script – Ordering Coffee in Sydney

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Hey there, welcome to Flat White & Co. How’s your morning going? What kind of coffee would you like to start with today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I’d like to order a coffee, please.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure thing! What kind of coffee are you in the mood for today? Something like an espresso, a flat white, or maybe a cappuccino?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think I’ll have a flat white, please.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect choice! A flat white is creamy and smooth. Would you like that in a small, medium, or large size? And do you have a preference for the type of milk — full cream, skim, or maybe a dairy-free option?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: A medium one with soy milk, please.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it! So, a medium flat white with soy milk. Would you like to add any sweeteners or a flavor shot, like vanilla or caramel?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. I’ll have a caramel flavor shot.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great! So, a medium flat white with soy milk and caramel flavor — that sounds delicious! Will you be having it here or taking it to go?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No worries. And how would you like to pay today — cash or card?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect! So, that’s a medium flat white with soy milk, caramel flavor, takeaway, and you’ll be paying by card. All set! ☕ Feedback Section

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You did really well! Your responses were clear and polite. Just a couple of small improvements: Instead of saying “I want soy milk”, try “I’d like soy milk” or “I prefer soy milk.” When ordering, using “Could I have” or “I’d like to order” sounds a bit more natural. Overall, your English was great — confident and easy to understand!
